2605.07701.title: Guidance Is Not a Hyperparameter: Learning Dynamic Control in Diffusion Language Models
2605.07701.paper_summary: Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) is a widely used mechanism for controlling diffusion-based generative models, yet its guidance scale is typically treated as a fixed hyperparameter throughout generation. This static design yields a suboptimal controllability and quality tradeoff, as the optimal degree of guidance varies across tasks and across different stages of the diffusion process, especially in NLP domain. We recast CFG scale selection as a sequential decision-making problem and propose to learn dynamic guidance trajectories via reinforcement learning. Specifically, we model the guidance scale as a discrete control action selected at each generation step based on the evolving diffusion state, and optimize a policy using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) under task-level rewards. Experiments on three controlled NLP generation tasks using discrete diffusion language models demonstrate that adaptive guidance consistently achieves a better balance between controllability and generation quality than fixed-scale strategies. Further analysis of the learned policies reveals distinct and interpretable guidance trajectories across tasks, underscoring the importance of treating guidance as a dynamic control process rather than a static design choice.
2605.07701.paper_summary_zh: 分類器自由引導（CFG）是一種廣泛使用的機制，用於控制基於擴散的生成模型，但其引導比例通常在生成過程中被視為固定的超參數。這種靜態設計導致可控性和質量之間的權衡不理想，因為最佳的引導程度在不同任務和擴散過程的不同階段之間有所變化，尤其是在自然語言處理領域。我們將CFG比例選擇重新定義為一個序列決策問題，並提出通過強化學習來學習動態引導軌跡。具體而言，我們將引導比例建模為在每個生成步驟中根據不斷變化的擴散狀態選擇的離散控制行動，並在任務層級的獎勵下使用近端策略優化（PPO）來優化策略。在三個控制的自然語言處理生成任務中進行的實驗表明，自適應引導在可控性和生成質量之間始終能夠達到比固定比例策略更好的平衡。對學習到的策略進一步分析顯示，不同任務之間存在明確且可解釋的引導軌跡，強調了將引導視為動態控制過程而非靜態設計選擇的重要性。
2605.07701.author: Fan Zhou et.al.
2605.07701.authors: Fan Zhou, Tim Van de Cruys
